title: Ligand-confined zinc slurries enable durable flow-based energy storage
abstract: Published online: 13 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02096-5 Zinc-based batteries could offer long-duration energy storage, yet conventional zinc electrodes degrade during cycling. Now, a flowing zinc slurry battery is developed with zinc nanoparticles dispersed in a conductive network and a ligand that stabilizes the slurry. The slurry shows reversible Zn/Zn2+ conversion and enables durable battery operation.

title: Nanoengineered doping overcomes sintering and grain-boundary limitations in <mark>all-solid-state</mark> <mark>lithium</mark> batteries with <mark>garnet</mark> electrolytes
abstract: Published online: 13 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02226-w Robust and lithiophobic interphases enhance grain-boundary strength in inorganic solid-state electrolytes and stabilize the interfaces of <mark>lithium</mark> metal and positive electrodes, enabling effective operation of <mark>all-solid-state</mark> batteries
